player_private_info_dict.reason:
The opponent has proposed giving me 10 Y for 15 X.
Given my current surplus of 25 X and only 5 Y, this trade is favorable for me because I gain Y (which I currently lack) while only losing a moderate amount of X (which I have in excess).

However, since this is the final round, I should aim to extract maximum value from the trade. The opponent claims to have a surplus in Y, so they likely have more Y than X.
Their proposal (15 X for 10 Y) suggests they are willing to part with 10 Y, which is likely their upper limit since they want more X.

I should push slightly harder to secure more Y for fewer X. I will counter with a proposal that gives 12 X for 10 Y. This maintains a good trade ratio for me, since I gain Y and only lose a small portion of my X surplus.

If they reject this, I will then accept their initial offer (15 X for 10 Y) as it still provides a favorable trade for me.
